channel.title: NPR Topics: Economy
channel.description: NPR news on the U.S. and world economy, the World Bank, and Federal Reserve. Commentary on economic trends. Subscribe to NPR Economy podcasts and RSS feeds.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: How does the economy shape our lives? Planet Money has a book for that!
item.description: NPR's Planet Money is publishing its first book! The team's newest project aims to help audiences navigate the economy and their lives.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Planet Money is publishing its first book! The team's newest project aims to help audiences navigate the economy and their lives.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Gonzalez

item.title: Beer cans, helium balloons and mortgages: An unexpected mix of things affected by war
item.description: It's not just oil and gas that are affected by the Iran war. All sorts of shortages and price spikes are starting to pop up that stand to affect people's daily lives.
item.content:encoded:
It's not just oil and gas that are affected by the Iran war. All sorts of shortages and price spikes are starting to pop up that stand to affect people's daily lives.

(Image credit: Brent Jones)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: An analysis of new U.S. employment growth data
item.description: New data from the Labor Department shows stronger than expected jobs growth. NPR's Scott Simon asks Heather Long, chief economist at Navy Federal Credit Union, for her reaction.
item.content:encoded: New data from the Labor Department shows stronger than expected jobs growth. NPR's Scott Simon asks Heather Long, chief economist at Navy Federal Credit Union, for her reaction.
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: When legal sports betting surges, so do Americans' financial problems
item.description: As online betting has grown in popularity, a new report from the New York Federal Reserve builds on the troubling link between legal sports wagering and financial health.
item.content:encoded:
As online betting has grown in popularity, a new report from the New York Federal Reserve builds on the troubling link between legal sports wagering and financial health.

(Image credit: Charlie Riedel)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: The labor market springs back to life in March as employers add 178,000 jobs
item.description: The U.S. job market perked up last month as employers added 178,000 jobs. The unemployment rate dipped to 4.3%, mainly because the number of people seeking work declined.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. job market perked up last month as employers added 178,000 jobs. The unemployment rate dipped to 4.3%, mainly because the number of people seeking work declined.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: War with Iran continues, raising big concerns across the economy and markets
item.description: Financial markets were not reassured by President Trump's address to the nation. Stocks tumbled while energy prices climbed. The shooting war adds to the economic fallout from Trump's trade war.
item.content:encoded: Financial markets were not reassured by President Trump's address to the nation. Stocks tumbled while energy prices climbed. The shooting war adds to the economic fallout from Trump's trade war.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Have Trump's tariffs worked? This is where things stand a year after 'Liberation Day'
item.description: It has been a year since President Trump announced double-digit tariffs on imports from around the world. So far, those levies have not produced the economic boom the president promised.
item.content:encoded:
It has been a year since President Trump announced double-digit tariffs on imports from around the world. So far, those levies have not produced the economic boom the president promised.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: How long the Strait of Hormuz stays closed will have major economic implications
item.description: What are the implications for the oil market and the broader global economy if the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed for months?
item.content:encoded: What are the implications for the oil market and the broader global economy if the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed for months?
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: The average U.S. gas price is now $4 a gallon. What does that mean for the economy?
item.description: The average national price of a gallon of gas has passed the $4 mark for the first time in more than three years.
item.content:encoded: The average national price of a gallon of gas has passed the $4 mark for the first time in more than three years.
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Gas crosses $4 a gallon in the U.S. for the first time in 3 years
item.description: The war with Iran has driven up gas prices at a time when affordability is high on people's minds.
item.content:encoded:
The war with Iran has driven up gas prices at a time when affordability is high on people's minds.

(Image credit: Ronaldo Schemidt)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha
